jokes.text: Free beer. Free beer. Free beer-beer-beer-beer-beer- beer-beer-beer-beer-beer! Free...!
jokes.setup: Character excitedly chanting about free beer
jokes.punchline: The escalating repetition and implied frantic behavior
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender behavior - his obsession with free stuff drives manic repetition. Character-appropriate but structurally simple.

jokes.text: Hello. I'm here for the free beer. You got it. Right after these 800 people get it.
jokes.punchline: Massive line ahead reveals the 'free' beer isn't so immediate
jokes.explanation: Clean setup/punchline structure with good economy. The subversion of expectation works well.

jokes.text: Thank you, headless clone of Agnew
jokes.punchline: Casually referring to a 'headless clone of Agnew' as if it's normal
jokes.explanation: Brilliant throwaway line that establishes the absurd sci-fi world. Perfect economy and wonderfully bizarre imagery.

jokes.text: the greatest planet in the world
jokes.punchline: Calling Earth 'the greatest planet in the world' - nonsensical phrasing
jokes.explanation: Nixon's characteristic bumbling speech patterns. The logical error is perfectly in character and subtly delivered.

jokes.text: Is everyone enjoying their free beer? Well, there's plenty more where that came from. We're all out of beer.
jokes.punchline: Immediate contradiction that they're out
jokes.explanation: Classic political hypocrisy joke with perfect timing. The immediate contradiction creates strong ironic impact.

jokes.text: a really big Dyson fence across the Southern border of our solar system
jokes.punchline: A 'Dyson fence' (vacuum brand) around the solar system
jokes.explanation: Clever brand name pun that works on multiple levels - vacuum/space connection plus absurd scale. Original and well-crafted.

jokes.text: use the poor as a cheap source of teeth for aquarium gravel
jokes.punchline: Horrifically specific and bizarre use for poor people's teeth
jokes.explanation: Darkly brilliant absurdist humor. The specificity of 'aquarium gravel' makes it memorably disturbing and hilarious.

jokes.text: Why are you cheering, Fry? You're not rich. True, but someday I might be rich. And then people like me better watch their step.
jokes.setup: Fry cheering for anti-poor policies despite being poor
jokes.punchline: His illogical reasoning about future wealth
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry logic - optimistically naive and completely backwards. Sharp political satire about temporarily embarrassed millionaires.

jokes.text: I've repaired myself with these convenient 'Reelect Nixon' ass stickers
jokes.punchline: Using political stickers as patches, calling them 'ass stickers'
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender resourcefulness with crude language. The visual of political stickers as robot patches is amusing.

jokes.text: Convicted, sentenced and executed
jokes.setup: Bender being called a convicted felon
jokes.punchline: Escalating to 'and executed' as if it's no big deal
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation with excellent economy. Bender's casual delivery of being executed is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: I'll stick with the evil maniac I know, thank you
jokes.punchline: Openly calling him an evil maniac while supporting him
jokes.explanation: Sharp political satire about voter apathy and lesser-of-evils thinking. Clean delivery with good economy.

jokes.text: What?
jokes.setup: Leela announces she'll get involved in politics
jokes.punchline: Everyone's shocked silence/reaction
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction beat. Works as character moment but not strong comedy.

jokes.text: Welcome to debate 3012, the 3,012th debate of the 3012 election year
jokes.punchline: Absurdly high number of debates (3,012 debates in one year)
jokes.explanation: Subtle satire of endless political debates. The absurd number works as commentary on media oversaturation.

jokes.text: I will dispense with the informal terrorizing
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning 'terrorizing' as a normal debate element
jokes.explanation: Brilliant throwaway line that implies debates normally include terrorizing. Perfect deadpan delivery with dark edge.

jokes.text: We have a saying up in Alaska. That's all.
jokes.setup: Candidate beginning to share Alaskan wisdom
jokes.punchline: Completely abandoning the thought mid-sentence
jokes.explanation: Masterful misdirection with perfect economy. Brilliant parody of empty political rhetoric and poor public speaking.

jokes.text: A thoughtful and lucid answer. You will be destroyed!
jokes.punchline: Immediately threatening destruction for being thoughtful
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of how substantive politicians get punished. The juxtaposition of praise and threat is perfectly timed.

jokes.text: Two words: Condor attack
jokes.punchline: Fear-mongering about condor attacks as environmental threat
jokes.explanation: Absurdist political fear-mongering parody. The randomness of 'condor attack' works as political absurdity.

jokes.text: Enviromite!
jokes.setup: Travers trying to give serious environmental answer
jokes.punchline: Crowd shouting made-up insult 'Enviromite'
jokes.explanation: Clever portmanteau of 'environment' and 'termite/mite' as a political slur. Good wordplay with political satire edge.

jokes.text: I never should have used the same pr guy as one hour hot dog
jokes.setup: Travers lamenting his poor campaign performance
jokes.punchline: Comparing his PR to a fast food joint called 'one hour hot dog'
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific business name that's inherently funny - one hour for a hot dog is absurdly slow. Great worldbuilding humor.

jokes.text: We just need to conceal your intelligence and honesty in a hipper, dumber package
jokes.punchline: Suggesting they hide his good qualities to win
jokes.explanation: Sharp political satire about dumbing down for electoral success. Leela's earnest delivery of cynical advice works perfectly.

jokes.text: caught with a dead girl, a live boy or any kind of sexy ghost
jokes.setup: Describing when the runner-up would take over
jokes.punchline: Escalating from traditional political scandals to 'sexy ghost'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from classic political scandal reference to absurd 'sexy ghost.' The specificity makes it memorable.

jokes.text: It wasn't 'we the people'. It was you the mutant
jokes.punchline: Hermes pointing out it was specifically Leela as a mutant
jokes.explanation: Good character moment that deflates Leela's idealism with practical reality. Works with character dynamics.

jokes.text: Your insightful nagging really won us over
jokes.punchline: Calling it 'insightful nagging' - backhanded compliment
jokes.explanation: Classic Professor's backhanded compliment style. The contradiction between 'insightful' and 'nagging' works well.

jokes.text: I'm 40% wire
jokes.setup: Asked if he's wearing a wire
jokes.punchline: Bender's literal interpretation about his robot composition
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to Bender's running gag about his composition percentages. Excellent economy and character integration.

jokes.text: Nobel prize? That's no good
jokes.punchline: Disappointed that he won a Nobel Prize instead of having scandals
jokes.explanation: Good ironic reversal where accomplishments are bad news for opposition research. Clean political satire.

jokes.text: Well, you did manage to get a tremendous urine sample
jokes.setup: Nixon noting Bender's accomplishments in opposition research
jokes.punchline: Praising him for getting a large urine sample instead of useful intel
jokes.explanation: Mildly crude humor about incompetent spying. Works as character comedy but relies somewhat on grossness.

jokes.text: He's as clean as a freshly waxed buttock
jokes.punchline: Using the oddly specific comparison of a waxed buttock
jokes.explanation: Classic Lrrr's alien perspective creating weird analogies. The specificity of 'waxed' makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Hey! Did you put your tongue in my ear? Certainly not. I don't have a tongue. Oh, good. 'cause I don't have an ear.
jokes.punchline: Both characters revealing they lack the relevant body parts
jokes.explanation: Good escalating absurdity that resolves in mutual impossibility. Works with both characters' non-human nature.

jokes.text: His middle name is Zaxxar? Sounds kind of alien
jokes.punchline: The obviously alien-sounding name creating suspicion
jokes.explanation: Sets up the birther conspiracy plot. The name is appropriately alien-sounding for the premise.

jokes.text: I feel a jowl movement coming on
jokes.setup: Nixon getting excited about dirty politics
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'bowel movement' using his distinctive jowls
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character-specific pun that plays on Nixon's physical features and crude humor. Perfect character integration.

jokes.text: You Ruth Bader believe it!
jokes.punchline: Ruth Bader Ginsburg making pun on her own name
jokes.explanation: Clever name pun that works both as wordplay and character moment. The self-referential aspect is amusing.

jokes.text: fox quote-unquote news
jokes.punchline: Saying 'quote-unquote' around 'news' implying it's not real news
jokes.explanation: Sharp media satire with good economy. The self-aware criticism of Fox News works well.

jokes.text: Our lady of patriotism hospital
jokes.punchline: Hospital with overly patriotic religious name
jokes.explanation: Good worldbuilding humor mixing religious hospital naming with patriotic themes. Appropriately absurd.

jokes.text: Why are you yokels doing this?
jokes.punchline: Using 'yokels' instead as if it's less offensive
jokes.explanation: Mild callback that shows Leela's attempt at political correctness while still being insulting. Decent character moment.

jokes.text: We don't want a foreigner! We want someone born here! Ah, I'm still working on that one.
jokes.punchline: Admitting he hasn't figured out the rhyme yet
jokes.explanation: Good meta-joke about the mechanics of protest chants. The honesty about struggling with rhyming is endearing.

jokes.text: your mother was admitted to the maternity ward... this morning!
jokes.setup: Looking for birth certificate leads to time paradox
jokes.punchline: His mother is giving birth to him right now, despite him being an adult
jokes.explanation: Excellent sci-fi comedy setup for time travel paradox. The revelation is perfectly timed and absurd.

jokes.text: The fact is, I really was born on earth... tomorrow.
jokes.setup: Admitting he wasn't born on Earth as expected
jokes.punchline: Reveals time travel twist - born tomorrow
jokes.explanation: Brilliant sci-fi twist that resolves the birther plot cleverly. Excellent economy and originality.

jokes.text: I really was born on earth... tomorrow
jokes.punchline: The logical impossibility of being born 'tomorrow'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant temporal paradox joke with perfect economy. The contradiction is both logical and absurd.

jokes.text: In this time of crisis, I call upon the soylent major... I mean silent majority
jokes.setup: Nixon making speech about feeding people
jokes.punchline: Accidentally saying 'soylent' (referencing Soylent Green - people as food)
jokes.explanation: Masterful dark comedy referencing Soylent Green. The 'slip of the tongue' reveals the horrific truth perfectly. Near-perfect execution.

jokes.text: Wait... I am a robot named b.B. Rodriguez. So, I become ruler of earth? Indeed. Yes! In your face, high school guidance counselor.
jokes.setup: Learning he becomes ruler of Earth
jokes.punchline: Gloating to his high school guidance counselor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender reaction - even becoming ruler of Earth is just a chance to spite authority figures. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: In your face, high school guidance counselor.
jokes.setup: Bender learning he becomes ruler of Earth
jokes.punchline: His petty revenge fantasy against school authority figure
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender response - takes world domination and makes it personal vendetta against minor authority figure.

jokes.text: All right, I'm low on bullets. Everybody scooch together.
jokes.punchline: Politely asking enemies to group up for efficient shooting
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy with polite understatement. The casual politeness in a violent situation is perfect.

jokes.text: If it's on TV, it has to be real
jokes.punchline: Fry's naive faith in television truth
jokes.explanation: Classic Fry naivety about media. Good character moment though not particularly original observation.

jokes.text: If it's on TV, it has to be real.
jokes.setup: Suggesting they broadcast Travers' birth live
jokes.punchline: Naive faith in television authenticity
jokes.explanation: Simple media satire about gullibility. Functional but not particularly original.

jokes.text: Linda, I'm down here at the pelvis, and folks round these parts can't remember the last time a time-traveling Senator attended his own birth
jokes.punchline: Treating time-traveling senator attending own birth as local color
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist humor treating impossible situation as routine news. The deadpan delivery makes it perfect.

jokes.text: Aw... he's got his own eyes
jokes.punchline: Since it's the same person, he literally has his own eyes
jokes.explanation: Clever play on common birth observation with time travel logic. The literal truth makes it funnier.

jokes.text: You stood by me and exposed my private medical records, even after I begged you not to
jokes.punchline: Thanking her for doing exactly what he asked her not to do
jokes.explanation: Good ironic thanks that highlights how invasive the whole process was. Solid political satire.

jokes.text: I don't give a bag of butts who won the election
jokes.punchline: Bender's crude dismissal with made-up curse
jokes.explanation: Typical Bender crude language and cynicism. The made-up swear 'bag of butts' is mildly amusing but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Nope. I don't give a bag of butts who won the election. Nothing's going to change.
jokes.setup: Someone asking if Bender's upset his candidate lost
jokes.punchline: Cynical dismissal of electoral impact with Bender-specific profanity
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific cynicism with Bender's unique profanity. Sets up the time paradox resolution.

jokes.text: It's politics 101
jokes.punchline: Treating complex sci-fi concept as basic politics knowledge
jokes.explanation: Good meta-joke about the show's sci-fi logic being treated as mundane. Solid delivery with economy.

jokes.text: Wow, it really doesn't matter who you vote for
jokes.punchline: Fry's naive conclusion about voting futility
jokes.explanation: Sharp political satire delivered with Fry's characteristic innocent observation. Good episode theme statement.

jokes.text: Nixon always wins!
jokes.setup: Nixon back in power after time reset
jokes.punchline: His triumphant declaration of inevitable victory
jokes.explanation: Good ending that reinforces the episode's theme about political inevitability. Classic Nixon bombast.
